description: The Habitat Garden has been designed to bring together native bushland character, wild meadow softness, and selective productive planting. A habitat-rich, low-chemical landscape that supports pollinators, small birds, butterflies, and local insect life while providing a calming daily outlook from the home. The planting palette prioritises Victorian natives, favouring species indigenous to the Surrey Hills area, selected for year-round flowering, seasonal movement, and layered ecological function. This forward-thinking design focuses on long-term resilience, sensory experience and supporting the re-establishment of local biodiversity.

habitat.planting_method: Layered ecological planting -- Victorian indigenous species

activity_log.title: Baseline assessment -- habitat garden installation
activity_log.notes: Baseline condition recorded. 20 indigenous species installed across ground, shrub and emerging canopy layers. Strong pollinator and small bird habitat intent. Surrey Hills EVC context -- Grassy Woodland -- acknowledged in species selection. Proximity to Surrey Hills Reserve noted as connectivity asset.
